content.cn_text: 第四部分
content.en_text: Section 4

content.cn_text: 您将听到学生关于佛罗里达州一项保育项目的演示文稿的一部分
content.en_text: You will hear part of a student presentation about a conservation project on the Florida

content.cn_text: 美国海岸。
content.en_text: Coast of the United States.

content.cn_text: 首先，您有时间查看第7页上的问题31到40。
content.en_text: First you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40, on page 7.

content.cn_text: 您
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听并回答问题31到40。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 今天我将谈论一个红树林再生项目的第一个年份。
content.en_text: Today I'm going to be talking about the first year of a regeneration project in a mangrove forest.

content.cn_text: 红树林生长在河口和海岸线，它们很重要，因为它们
content.en_text: Mangrove forests are found along river estuaries and coastlines and are important because they

content.cn_text: 通过在陆地和海洋之间充当屏障来防止洪水。
content.en_text: prevent flooding by acting as a barrier between the land and sea.

content.cn_text: 红树林有特殊的根，可以呼吸，使它们能在厚实、
content.en_text: The mangrove trees have special roots which can breathe and allow them to survive in thick,

content.cn_text: 不透气的泥浆中生存。
content.en_text: airless mud.

content.cn_text: 它们也是涉禽、鱼类和其他动物非常重要的栖息地。
content.en_text: They are also a very important habitat for waiting birds, fish and other animals.

content.cn_text: 在项目进行的地区，自该地区首次
content.en_text: In the area where the project is taking place, there have been a number of problems since the area was first

content.cn_text: 定居100多年前以来，出现了一些问题。
content.en_text: settled over 100 years ago.

content.cn_text: 许多红树林最初被当地农民当作柴火烧掉。
content.en_text: Many of the mangrove trees were initially burnt as firewood by local farmers.

content.cn_text: 红树林还受到定居者耕作方式的毒害，因为农民使用
content.en_text: The mangrove forests were also poisoned by settlers farming methods because the farmers used

content.cn_text: 肥料来增加作物产量，而这些肥料开始渗入水中，
content.en_text: fertilizer to increase crop yields and this started to seep into the water,

content.cn_text: 最终导致部分红树林死亡。
content.en_text: eventually killing part of the mangrove forest.

content.cn_text: 该地区的农业不成功，剩下的红树林区域
content.en_text: Farming in the area wasn't successful and what was left of the mangrove forest area

content.cn_text: 不受当地人重视，因为那里不能种植农作物。
content.en_text: wasn't valued by local people as crops couldn't be grown there.

content.cn_text: 因此，该地区开始被用作倾倒垃圾的地方。
content.en_text: And so what happened was that the area started to be used as somewhere to dump trash.

content.cn_text: 迫切需要采取行动来保护红树林并防止洪水。
content.en_text: Action was urgently needed to protect the mangrove forest and prevent flooding.

content.cn_text: 因此，红树林再生项目得以设立。
content.en_text: And so the mangrove regeneration project was set up.

content.cn_text: 参与其中的环保人士决定在森林周围建造一个沙障。
content.en_text: The conservationists involved decided to construct a sand barrier around the forests.

content.cn_text: 但不幸的是，这被证明是无效的。
content.en_text: But unfortunately this proved to be ineffective.

content.cn_text: 唯一的出路似乎是从种子开始种植新的红树林。
content.en_text: The only way forward appeared to be to grow new mangroves from seed.

content.cn_text: 美国的这个地区有几种红树林物种，
content.en_text: Several species of mangrove inhabit the forests in this area of the United States,

content.cn_text: 但我们这里关注的是灰海榄（灰红树）。
content.en_text: but it is the grey mangroves that we are concerned with here.

content.cn_text: 这种植物的种子大约杏仁大小，大多数种子只有在完全成熟时才会落下。
content.en_text: The seed of this plant is about the size of an almond and most seeds fall only when they are fully ripe.

content.cn_text: 红树林再生项目于三年前首次启动。
content.en_text: The mangrove regeneration project first began three years ago.

content.cn_text: 第一批幼苗被种在小盆里，并放在温室中发芽。
content.en_text: The first set of seedlings was planted in small pots and left to germinate in a hothouse.

content.cn_text: 植物生长茂盛，盆底长出了大根。
content.en_text: The plants thrived and large roots appeared at the bottom of the pots.

content.cn_text: 理想情况下，这些幼苗在被种植到海里之前，应该用逐渐增加盐度的水进行驯化。
content.en_text: Ideally, these seedlings should have been conditioned with increasingly salty water before being planted in the sea.

content.cn_text: 事实上，它们只被用雨水浇灌。
content.en_text: They had, in fact, only been watered with rain water.

content.cn_text: 由于这些植物不适应盐水环境，决定将它们种植在附近一个小岛的南侧。
content.en_text: As the plants weren't used to a saline environment, it was decided to plant them out on the south side of a small island nearby.

content.cn_text: 希望这样能让它们逐渐适应咸水，
content.en_text: It was hoped that this would allow them to get used to salt water gradually,

content.cn_text: 因为岛的这一部分每天涨潮时都会被淹没。
content.en_text: since this part of the island was flooded every day at high tide.

content.cn_text: 在这个特定的地点种植了100多株植物，
content.en_text: There were over 100 plants planted in this particular spot,

content.cn_text: 并且有必要保护它们，不是免受大量到该地区觅食的涉禽的侵害，
content.en_text: and it was necessary to protect them not from the large number of wading birds which visit the area looking for food,

content.cn_text: 而是免受居住在该地区的大型兔子群落的侵害。
content.en_text: but from the large rabbit colony living in the area.

content.cn_text: 第二批幼苗的培育过程完全不同。
content.en_text: The process for the second set of seedlings was completely different.

content.cn_text: 从森林中收集幼苗，然后运到一个新的地点。
content.en_text: Young seedlings were collected from the forests and then taken to a new site.

content.cn_text: 幼苗随后被种植在旧红树林根部后面的海床上以获得保护。
content.en_text: The seedlings were then planted in the sea bed behind old mangrove roots for protection.

content.cn_text: 然而，这种方法并没有非常成功，绝大多数幼苗在一次风暴中被冲走了。
content.en_text: However, this method did not prove very successful, and the vast majority of these seedlings were washed away in a storm.

content.cn_text: 幸运的是，第一批幼苗存活了下来，这个方法将是该项目未来继续使用的方法。
content.en_text: Luckily, the first set of seedlings survived, and this method is the one which the project will continue to use in the future.

content.cn_text: 现在，我想更详细地描述一下这个过程。
content.en_text: Now, I'd like to describe in more detail the process.

content.cn_text: 第四部分到此结束。
content.en_text: That is the end of section 4.

content.cn_text: 现在您有半分钟的时间来检查您的答案。
content.en_text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.

groups.desc:
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
groups.title: Questions 31–40

items.title: protect coastal areas from 31. __________ by the sea
items.analysis: 答案解析：第31题问红树林保护沿海地区免受什么侵害。原文指出红树林通过作为陆地和海洋之间的屏障来“防止洪水”（prevent flooding），因此“flooding”是正确答案。

items.title: mangroves had been used by farmers as 32. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第32题问农民将红树林用作什么。原文明确提到红树林“被当地农民当作柴火烧掉”（burnt as firewood by local farmers），因此“firewood”是正确答案。

items.title: mangroves were poisoned by the use of 33. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第33题问红树林被什么的使用所毒害。原文指出农民使用“肥料”（fertilizer）来增加作物产量，这些肥料渗入水中毒害了红树林，因此“fertilizer”是正确答案。

items.title: local people used the mangroves as a place to put their 34. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第34题问当地人将红树林用作什么地方。原文提到该区域开始被用作“倾倒垃圾”（dump trash）的地方，因此“trash”是正确答案。

items.title: a barrier which was made of 35. __________ was constructed – but it failed
items.analysis: 答案解析：第35题问建造的屏障是由什么制成的。原文明确指出环保人士建造了一个“沙障”（sand barrier），因此“sand”是正确答案。

items.title: the seeds of the 36. __________ mangrove were used
items.analysis: 答案解析：第36题问使用了哪种红树林的种子。原文明确指出他们关注的是“灰海榄”（grey mangroves），因此“grey”是正确答案。

items.title: kept in small pots in a 37. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第37题问第一批幼苗被放在小盆里，并在哪里发芽。原文提到幼苗被放在“温室”（hot house）中发芽，因此“hothouse”是正确答案。

items.title: watered with 38. __________ water
items.analysis: 答案解析：第38题问幼苗是用什么水浇灌的。原文明确指出幼苗只被“雨水”（rain water）浇灌，因此“rain”是正确答案。

items.title: at risk from the large 39. __________ population
items.analysis: 答案解析：第39题问第一批幼苗受到大型什么动物群落的威胁。原文明确指出需要保护幼苗“不是免受涉禽的侵害”（not from the large number of wading birds），而是“免受居住在该地区的大型兔子群落的侵害”（from the large rabbit colony living in the area）。因此，“rabbit”是正确答案，“wading birds”是干扰项。

items.title: the young plants were destroyed in a 40. __________
items.analysis: 答案解析：第40题问第二批幼苗被什么摧毁了。原文明确指出绝大多数幼苗在“一次风暴中被冲走了”（washed away in a storm），因此“storm”是正确答案。

groups.source_collect:
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
The Mangrove Regeneration Project
Mangrove forests:
• protect coastal areas from $31$ by the sea
• are an important habitat for wildlife
Problems:
• mangroves had been used by farmers as $32$
• mangroves were poisoned by the use of $33$
• local people used the mangroves as a place to put their $34$
Actions taken to protect the mangroves:
• a barrier which was made of $35$ was constructed – but it failed
• new mangroves had to be grown from seed
• the seeds of the $36$ mangrove were used
First set of seedlings:
• kept in small pots in a $37$
• watered with $38$ water
• planted out on south side of a small island
• at risk from the large $39$ population
Second set of seedlings:
• planted in the seabed near established mangrove roots
• the young plants were destroyed in a $40$
Results:
• The first set of seedlings was successful
